associatedMedia.headline: Balnagown Castle, Kildary, Eastern Ross, Scotland
associatedMedia.description: Balnagown Castle is located beside the village of Kildary in Easter Ross, part of the Highland area of Scotland. There has been a castle on the site since the 14th century, although the present building was remodeled in the 18th and 19th centuries. Balnagown is the ancestral home of the Chiefs of Clan Ross.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Cleireach the Clerk Macleod Vth of Dunvegan Vth Chief於1355年出生在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾登維根時，他的父親John, Ian Ciar MacLeod 4th Clan Chief25歲，母親Annie O'Neil15歲。他於大約1380年與Janet MacLean在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1402年在蘇格蘭享年47歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾。

nameMeanings.name: Macleod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: McLeod
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Leòid ‘son of Leòd’, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of Old Norse Liótr ‘ugly’, a common personal name in medieval Norway and Iceland.

History: This is the name of a major Scottish Hebridean clan, associated in particular with Dunvegan on the isle of Skye; also, historically, with the island of Lewis. There are two main branches: the McLeods of Harris and Dunvegan (Sìol Tormoid) and the McLeods of Lewis (Sìol Torcaill). Both branches claim descent from a certain Leòd (Norse: Liótr), who lived in the 13th century. — One prominent bearer of this prominent Scottish name, Alexander McLeod (1774–1833), a Reformed Presbyterian clergyman, author, and editor, emigrated to the US in 1792 from the island of Mull in the Hebrides.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Clerk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which originated as an occupational name denoting a clerk (Latin clericus), in the Middle Ages a man in minor holy orders who earned his living by his ability to read and write. It is recorded as a given name in Britain from the late 17th century onwards. Since the 1930s, when it was associated particularly with the film star Clark Gable ( 1901–60 ), it has been widely used as a given name, especially in North America.

variants.name: Clarke
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which originated as an occupational name denoting a clerk (Latin clericus), in the Middle Ages a man in minor holy orders who earned his living by his ability to read and write. It is recorded as a given name in Britain from the late 17th century onwards. Since the 1930s, when it was associated particularly with the film star Clark Gable ( 1901–60 ), it has been widely used as a given name, especially in North America.

en.lifeSummary: When William Cleireach the Clerk Macleod Vth of Dunvegan Vth Chief was born in 1355, in Dunvegan, Inverness-shire, Scotland, his father, John, Ian Ciar MacLeod 4th Clan Chief, was 25 and his mother, Annie O'Neil, was 15. He married Janet MacLean about 1380, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1402, in Scotland, at the age of 47, and was buried in Inverness-shire, Scotland.

Il a épousé Janet MacLean environ 1380, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1402, à Écosse, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Inverness-shire, Écosse.

ko.lifeSummary: William Cleireach the Clerk Macleod Vth of Dunvegan Vth Chief(이)가 1355년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 던베건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John, Ian Ciar MacLeod 4th Clan Chief(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Annie O'Neil(은)는 15세였다. 그는 대략 1380년 스컷랜드에서 Janet MacLean(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1402년 스컷랜드에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르에 묻혔다.
